title: Delicate Flaky Thai Chuck Roast Larb
description: A briny and flaky Thai dish featuring chuck roast in a larb form, served in a lacquerware bowl, perfect for a brunch mood. This diabetic-friendly dish is a twist on traditional Thai cuisine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together fish sauce, lime juice, and chili flakes.
Add the chuck roast strips to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Remove the chuck roast from the marinade and place it on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the chuck roast in the preheated oven for 2 hours, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the chuck roast is baking, heat a tablespoon of oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and bruised lemon grass to the pan and cook for 1 minute.
Once the chuck roast is done, let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing it into thin strips.
In a large bowl, combine the sliced chuck roast, cooked rice, chopped mint leaves, and chopped basil leaves.
Pour the garlic and lemon grass mixture over the top of the chuck roast mixture and toss to combine.
